text.title: Design Tokens Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying design-tokens. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Component files contain hex values like #1a2b3c instead of semantic tokens.
antipattern.root-cause: Designer handed off Figma values directly; engineer pasted them without going through tokens.
antipattern.fix: Add a lint rule that flags hex in component source files. Force every value through tokens.json. Re-author the offending components.

antipattern.symptom: iOS Swift constants and CSS vars disagree on the same semantic colour.
antipattern.root-cause: Engineer hand-edited the Swift output instead of the JSON source; regenerated CSS lacked the same change.
antipattern.fix: Move Style Dictionary build into CI; commit outputs from CI only. Mark generated dirs read-only locally; force changes to flow through JSON.

antipattern.symptom: Component tokens reference primitives directly (button-bg = blue-500 instead of surface/interactive).
antipattern.root-cause: Author shortcut the semantic tier 'just for one component'; pattern spread.
antipattern.fix: Refactor to add semantic aliases. Block PRs that introduce primitive→component references via lint.

antipattern.symptom: Figma variables and JSON tokens drift apart over months; designs and code show different brand.
antipattern.root-cause: No parity check; both sides edit independently.
antipattern.fix: Wire the figma-parity check from r5 into CI. Fail PRs that diverge unless ADR justifies.
